# qweave v0.4.1

This patch release publishes the renamed qweave project surface and the new
Chinese-first bilingual public documentation. It also records fresh benchmark
results from the current Windows/PowerShell environment.

## Highlights

- Project-facing documentation now uses `qweave` throughout.
- README and docs were rewritten to explain the project's advantages,
  performance design, and how it differs from Qlib and KunQuant.
- Added `docs/comparison.md` / `docs/comparison.en.md`.
- Release workflow now uses checked-in tag-specific release notes when present.

## Benchmark Environment

- Date: 2026-07-10
- Target: `v0.4.1`
- OS: Microsoft Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200
- CPU: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X 16-Core Processor
- Cores / logical processors: 16 / 32
- Memory: 61.7 GiB
- Python: 3.12.13 via `uv`
- Rust: `rustc 1.99.0-nightly (f10db292a 2026-07-07)`
- Cargo: `cargo 1.99.0-nightly (2f0e7011e 2026-07-05)`
- qweave build: `uv run --python 3.12 maturin develop --uv --release`
- Benchmark data: deterministic synthetic OHLCV panel, no real market data

## Benchmark Commands

```powershell
$env:TMP = "C:\qweave-bench-tmp"
$env:TEMP = "C:\qweave-bench-tmp"

uv run --python 3.12 --with pyqlib python scripts\bench_factor_engines.py --workload alpha158 --engines qweave,qlib --symbols 1000 --days 500 --repeats 3 --warmups 1 --threads 1 --json C:\qweave-bench-tmp\qweave-v0.4.1-alpha158.json

uv run --python 3.12 --with KunQuant --with setuptools python scripts\bench_factor_engines.py --workload worldquant101 --engines qweave,kunquant --symbols 1000 --days 500 --repeats 3 --warmups 1 --threads 1 --json C:\qweave-bench-tmp\qweave-v0.4.1-worldquant101.json
```

## Results

### Qlib Alpha158

500,000 rows, 158 factors, 3 measured repeats after 1 warmup.

| Engine | Best s | Mean s | Stdev s | Rows/s | Cells/s | Notes |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | --- |
| qweave | 0.1818 | 0.2337 | 0.0750 | 2,750,417 | 434,565,924 | `qlib_alpha158()` expression library |
| Qlib | 34.1608 | 34.3119 | 0.2023 | 14,637 | 2,312,590 | Qlib `Alpha158DL` over a generated local binary provider |

On this run, qweave's best Alpha158 wall time was about 188x lower than Qlib's
best wall time for the same synthetic panel and factor count.

### KunQuant WorldQuant101

500,000 rows, 82 comparable factors, 3 measured repeats after 1 warmup.

| Engine | Best s | Mean s | Stdev s | Rows/s | Cells/s | Compile s | Notes |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | --- |
| qweave | 0.2796 | 0.2937 | 0.0244 | 1,788,560 | 146,661,921 | - | `worldquant_alpha101()` expression library |
| KunQuant | 5.3045 | 5.3888 | 0.0871 | 94,260 | 7,729,281 | 2.8940 | Elapsed time includes compile + run |

On this run, qweave's best WorldQuant101 wall time was about 19x lower than
KunQuant's best compile+run wall time for the same synthetic panel and factor
count. KunQuant's `compile_s` is reported separately because compiling a fresh
expression bundle is part of the end-to-end user experience.

## Notes

- These numbers are machine- and version-specific benchmark results, not a
  cross-environment guarantee.
- Historical macOS measurements are intentionally not carried forward after the
  Windows migration.
- The benchmark script is `scripts/bench_factor_engines.py`.
